meta_title: For Founders: API integration guide to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: API Integration Guide to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your lack of portfolio. Learn to master API integrations using Antigravity and Claude Code to build high-value SaaS products quickly.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, stop building toy apps and start shipping functional API integrations. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can deploy a revenue-ready MVP that demonstrates real-world technical competency and business value to potential customers or investors immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a founder’s worst enemy. You have the vision, but your GitHub profile is a digital ghost town, and potential clients are rightfully skeptical. You aren't just missing a portfolio; you're missing the proof of execution that separates dreamers from profitable SaaS founders. The gap isn't your coding ability—it's your speed to market.

We are going to bridge that gap by building a production-grade API integration. This isn't a tutorial; it's a tactical strike on your obscurity. By automating the boilerplate and focusing on business logic, you will transform your GitHub from an empty void into a library of high-value, functional assets.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Identify a specific API (e.g., Stripe, OpenAI, or Twilio) that solves a pain point for your niche. Do not build a 'wrapper'; build a utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project architecture for a SaaS micro-service that integrates the Stripe API to handle recurring billing workflows with error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a new project structure focusing on Stripe API integration using TypeScript.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Integration Layer
steps.description: Use Antigravity to create a clean, modular service layer for your API requests to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular API client class for the target integration with built-in retry logic, logging, and environment variable configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a service/api-client.ts file with robust error handling and type definitions.'

steps.title: Implement Business Logic
steps.description: Connect your API layer to a functional UI or command-line interface that demonstrates real-world utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the business logic to process API responses and update a database schema based on the integration requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the API client with the main application flow to process user requests.'

steps.title: Add Production-Grade Observability
steps.description: Add logging and monitoring to prove your code is reliable, which is a key trait of a senior developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a logging middleware that tracks API latency and failure rates, outputting to a structured format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add Winston logging middleware to all API endpoints.'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: Deploy your MVP and write a 'Technical Brief' README that explains the business value of the integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that highlights the problem solved, the API architecture used, and the deployment strategy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a comprehensive README.md that explains the project architecture and integration flow.'

pro_tips: Focus on the 'Edge Cases' in your README; showing you handled API timeouts is more impressive than showing you simply fetched data.
Use mock servers (like Prism) during development to keep your API costs at zero while you iterate on the logic.
Document your failures in the commit history; showing a fix for a complex bug proves you have real-world engineering experience.
Always wrap third-party API calls in an abstraction layer; never let raw SDK code touch your core business logic.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Call' in your project documentation; investors want to see how fast your app provides value.
content.next_steps: After completing this integration, publish it to a live demo site, write a 300-word post on LinkedIn explaining the business problem you solved, and link it directly to your portfolio site.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting a lack of portfolio hold your SaaS business back. You have the tools, the guide, and the urgency to build your way out of obscurity. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
